bullets.text: US President Trump has warned Taiwan against declaring independence, following a summit with China's Xi Jinping, urging both sides to de-escalate tensions.
An Ebola outbreak in the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo has claimed 65 lives, with Uganda also reporting cases of the Bundibugyo strain.
Federal Reserve Chair Powell indicated that a rate cut could be considered as early as September, stating that the labor market is not a significant source of inflation pressure.
Israel and Lebanon have agreed to extend a ceasefire and hold new talks on a political settlement, despite Israel carrying out new strikes it insists are not subject to the truce.
The federal government has announced nearly $40 million to fund an overhaul of aviation consumer rights, as the sector warns of higher fares and cancelled flights.
The discovery of a Chinese undersea monitoring device in a strategic Indonesian waterway last month highlights China's extensive reach and undersea ambitions, with implications for Australia.

Global bond markets experienced a significant downturn, with yields surging worldwide, driven by fears that escalating conflict in the Middle East could lead to an inflation shock and force central banks, including the US Federal Reserve, to raise interest rates further.
SpaceX is reportedly planning to file publicly for its initial public offering (IPO) as early as Wednesday, according to sources familiar with the matter.
An incident at EY saw the professional services firm retract a study after researchers discovered instances of AI hallucinations, highlighting challenges with new technology.
